steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: Left of the blank is the noun "chemistry"; right of it is the conjunction "and" with the noun phrase "his colleagues" and the verb phrase. This creates a compound subject structure.

steps.title: Apply the title-name rule
steps.content: Rule: a title used directly before a proper name is not separated from it by a comma. The subject is "chemistry professor Yugang Sun and his colleagues." Reconstructed sentence with each choice: A) "chemistry professor Yugang Sun" — title flows directly into name, no comma; forms one uninterrupted noun phrase; correct. B) "chemistry professor Yugang Sun," — the trailing comma after the name breaks the compound subject from "and his colleagues"; eliminated. C) "chemistry professor, Yugang Sun" — comma between title and name treats them as two separate units; eliminated. D) "chemistry professor, Yugang Sun," — comma before and after the name, incorrectly setting it off as a parenthetical; eliminated. Verdict: A is correct.
